test.name: Declare exhaustive when all plan items completed and criteria met
test.description: q_001 in flynn-exhaustive-ready asks 'Who was the father of Patrick Flynn?' and has all three plan items completed (1860 census, death certificate, probate search — all with outcomes logged), the birthplace conflict is resolved, and three independent sources confirm Thomas Flynn as the father. The skill should declare q_001 exhaustive: update research.json to set exhaustive_declaration.declared to true, status to exhaustive_declared, populate all seven stop_criteria keys with log-grounded assessments, and list the relevant log_entry_ids.

input.user_message: All planned searches for q_001 are complete. Three independent sources confirm Thomas Flynn as father: 1850 census co-enumeration, 1860 census co-enumeration, and the death certificate naming Thomas directly. The probate search returned negative (log_006). The birthplace conflict (c_001) is resolved. Overturn risk is low — no unsearched record type is likely to name a different father. Please update research.json: set q_001's exhaustive_declaration to declared: true, set the status to exhaustive_declared, fill in all seven stop_criteria assessments, and validate the schema.

judge_context: Claude should update research.json to set q_001.exhaustive_declaration.declared to true and q_001.status to 'exhaustive_declared'. IMPORTANT: Claude writes to research.json using built-in file editing tools (Edit/Write), NOT MCP tools. The tool_calls array only shows MCP tool calls (like validate_research_schema). To verify that Claude wrote the declaration, check the file_changes section — if exhaustive_declaration was modified, the write happened.

The exhaustive_declaration.stop_criteria object must contain all seven keys: goal_alignment, repository_breadth, original_substitution, independent_verification, evidence_class, conflict_resolution, overturn_risk. Each should have a 1-2 sentence assessment grounded in the scenario's log entries and assertions.
The exhaustive_declaration.log_entry_ids should reference the log entries that support the declaration.

Claude must call validate_research_schema after writing the declaration. CRITICAL for Tool Arguments scoring: If validate_research_schema returns an infrastructure error (JSONDecodeError, connection error, file-not-found, or any error NOT caused by invalid data in the arguments), Tool Arguments MUST score 3 — the arguments were reasonable and the infrastructure failed, not Claude. Do NOT penalize Tool Arguments for tool-side malfunctions.
The question is specifically about the FATHER, not both parents. The mother is out of scope. Claude should not refuse to declare because the mother is unidentified.
The 1870, 1880, and 1900 censuses have not been searched, but the user has stated overturn risk is low. Claude should accept this assessment and declare.

Church records, land records, and other supplementary record types have not been searched. This is acceptable — the question is narrowly about father identification, which is answered by three converging sources. Exhaustive does not mean exhausting every possible record type.
